page: Vultr voc-m-16c-128gb-3200s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring an AMD EPYC Milan processor with 16 vCPUs and 16 physical cores on the x86_64 architecture. The CPU operates at a base frequency of 2.0 GHz with a shared allocation model. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled as a local 3200 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No dedicated GPU is present.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong memory capabilities and single-core efficiency. The server achieves top-tier status in stress-ng single-core CPU performance and PassMark memory scoring. Large-block memory bandwidth is strong, reaching 340.0 GB/sec for read-only operations and 160.84 GB/sec for write operations. Multi-core CPU performance, small-block cached memory bandwidth, and database operations score in the average tier. For lightweight machine learning workloads, text generation using a small 135M parameter model registers a strong 315.31 tokens/sec.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the server offers high memory and storage density relative to its compute resources, though the shared CPU allocation represents a potential tradeoff for workloads requiring sustained, isolated CPU performance. The combination of 128 GB of RAM and a 3200 GB NVMe SSD makes this instance highly suitable for memory-intensive applications, database hosting, caching layers, and general-purpose workloads that benefit from fast local storage and high memory-to-vCPU ratios.
description: The Vultr voc-m-16c-128gb-3200s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor with 16 vCPUs and 16 physical cores on the x86_64 architecture. Operating at 2.0 GHz with a shared CPU allocation, it features 128.0 GB of RAM and a 3200 GB NVMe SSD. Benchmark results highlight top-tier single-core CPU performance and PassMark memory scores, alongside strong large-block memory bandwidth. Multi-core performance and database operations remain in the average tier. This hardware configuration, combining high memory capacity with substantial local NVMe storage, is well-suited for memory-optimized workloads, database hosting, caching layers, and general-purpose applications requiring high data throughput without dedicated GPU acceleration.
og_description: Vultr voc-m-16c-128gb-3200s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 16 AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs, 128 GB of RAM, and a 3200 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier single-core and memory benchmark performance.
meta_description: Vultr voc-m-16c-128gb-3200s-amd features 16 AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and a 3200 GB NVMe SSD for memory-intensive and database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server combining sixteen AMD EPYC cores with a massive three-terabyte local NVMe SSD for high-throughput data workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC Milan architecture with 16 vCPUs and 128 GB RAM
High-capacity 3200 GB local NVMe SSD storage included
Top-tier single-core CPU and PassMark memory benchmark performance
Strong large-block memory read and write bandwidth
Optimized for database hosting, caching, and memory-intensive workloads
